{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | native_name = 中塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.46727753|N|135.8757782|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakatsuka Kofun'''|中塚古墳| Nakatsuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161138 |title= 中塚古墳 |trans-title= Nakatsuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakatsuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway and near a hill called (Zenbuyama, 膳部山). The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The Nakatsuka Kofun is in relatively poor preservation, as much has been destroyed over the centuries by agricultural activity and as a source of soil. The tumulus has a two-tier structure with ''[[fukiishi]]'', orientated north-south, with the posterior circular portion on the northern end. The total length is 72 meters and the circular portion has a diameter of 46 meters and height of six meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 26 meters. Some fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity; however, the structure of the tumulus has never been properly [[archaeological excavation]] excavated by archaeologists and the inner structure is unknown; however, a {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} survey in 2008 indicated the presence of a {{ill|Passage grave|en|Passage grave|WD=Q1426772|lt=Passage grave|1=Passage grave}} [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] similar to that of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. Traces of a shield-shaped moat have also been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus dates from the late 5th century to early 6th century AD. The name of the person interred is unknown; however from the name of the nearby hill (Zenbuyama, 膳部山), it mostly likely corresponds to the grave of a head of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), who are recorded in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as having served as ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} since the time of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|WD=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | Nakatsuka Kofun || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-nakatsuka.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | native_name = 中塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.46727753|N|135.8757782|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakatsuka Kofun'''|中塚古墳| Nakatsuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|WD=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161138 |title= 中塚古墳 |trans-title= Nakatsuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakatsuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway and near a hill called (Zenbuyama, 膳部山). The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The Nakatsuka Kofun is in relatively poor preservation, as much has been destroyed over the centuries by agricultural activity and as a source of soil. The tumulus has a two-tier structure with ''[[fukiishi]]'', orientated north-south, with the posterior circular portion on the northern end. The total length is 72 meters and the circular portion has a diameter of 46 meters and height of six meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 26 meters. Some fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity; however, the structure of the tumulus has never been properly [[archaeological excavation]] excavated by archaeologists and the inner structure is unknown; however, a {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|WD=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} survey in 2008 indicated the presence of a {{ill|Passage grave|en|Passage grave|WD=Q1426772|lt=Passage grave|1=Passage grave}} [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] similar to that of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. Traces of a shield-shaped moat have also been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus dates from the late 5th century to early 6th century AD. The name of the person interred is unknown; however from the name of the nearby hill (Zenbuyama, 膳部山), it mostly likely corresponds to the grave of a head of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), who are recorded in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as having served as ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} since the time of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|WD=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|WD=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | Nakatsuka Kofun || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa)]][[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakatsuka Kofun]][[ja:中塚古墳 (若狭町)]][[zh:中塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakatsuka Kofun | native_name = 中塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakatsuka Kofun (Wakasa), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.46727753|N|135.8757782|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakatsuka Kofun'''|中塚古墳| Nakatsuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161138 |title= 中塚古墳 |trans-title= Nakatsuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakatsuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of central Wakasa, forming the Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster. It is west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway and near a hill called (Zenbuyama, 膳部山). The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The Nakatsuka Kofun is in relatively poor preservation, as much has been destroyed over the centuries by agricultural activity and as a source of soil. The tumulus has a two-tier structure with ''[[fukiishi]]'', orientated north-south, with the posterior circular portion on the northern end. The total length is 72 meters and the circular portion has a diameter of 46 meters and height of six meters, and the anterior rectangular portion has a width of 26 meters. Some fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found in the vicinity; however, the structure of the tumulus has never been properly [[archaeological excavation]] excavated by archaeologists and the inner structure is unknown; however, a {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|qid=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}} survey in 2008 indicated the presence of a {{ill|Passage grave|en|Passage grave|qid=Q1426772|lt=Passage grave|1=Passage grave}} [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] similar to that of the [[Jōnozuka Kofun]]. Traces of a shield-shaped moat have also been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus dates from the late 5th century to early 6th century AD. The name of the person interred is unknown; however from the name of the nearby hill (Zenbuyama, 膳部山), it mostly likely corresponds to the grave of a head of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), who are recorded in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as having served as ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} since the time of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|qid=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | [[Jōnozuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | Nakatsuka Kofun || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|qid=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}

The tumulus dates from the late 5th century to early 6th century AD. The name of the person interred is unknown; however from the name of the nearby hill (Zenbuyama, 膳部山), it mostly likely corresponds to the grave of a head of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), who are recorded in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as having served as ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} since the time of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|qid=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
